summary: Not ratifiable as-is. The interlock is fail-closed today because `registry/reuse-ratification.json` is absent, but ratifying this revision would still leave critical reuse-permissive gaps. RC-7 is mostly a labeled pinned-file check, not proof that the comparator rule predates unblinding or is substantively data-blind. RC-4/RC-2/Case-A mechanics still allow already-seen or non-identical data to reach a confirmatory analysis under plausible declarations.

amendment_closure.note: The code requires `comparator_selection`, basis enum, ref path+sha, and non-empty rule text, but it does not prove the ref is prior-frozen, that the rule text is actually the referenced law, or that `exhaustive-declared-family` is exhaustive. A post-unblinding file with a lawful-looking basis label can pass.

`outcome_selected_arms:true` refuses without `selection_adjusted_inference` anchored to pinned analysis output fields; ASM-0010 is rewritten `load_bearing:true`. The declaration remains attested, not auto-detected.

Overlap coverage is per block/producer and per consumed arm-rung stratum, with frozen representativeness justifications for misses. The deterministic CPU waiver requires a pinned `consumed_output_hashes` artifact and recomputes per-row consumed-field hashes.

amendment_closure.enforcing_ref: registry/schema/kot-reg-2.json:102-184; registry/schema/kot-log-1.json:50-59; tools/registry/prereg-freeze.py:280-304; tools/registry/log-append.py:114-147; tools/registry/verdict-gen.py:376-426; tools/registry/registry-check.py:288-365
amendment_closure.note: The wire fields, witness, freeze collision refusal, and verdict-time consumption checks landed. Gaps remain: `registry-check --reuse` does not perform collision refusal, recheck/audit uses freeze-like RC-2 semantics, and ratification may pin an arbitrary `plan_path` instead of this ruling document.

Record mode derives live rows and exits 3 with `--gate` on uncovered identical/indeterminate cells. However the renamed-identical surfacing is only heuristic and does not compare declared new arm impl pins against old-arm logged rows unless a same-named logged row provides the impl value; ad-hoc mode is still broader discovery, not the RC gate.
ASM-0010 is load-bearing and removes the f7 default. `reuse-check lscore` derives L from structured experiment/idea edges and, in this repo, reports 17 free-text mismatches.

new_loopholes.description: RC-4 fresh-arm enforcement can be satisfied by an irrelevant fresh arm while the primary analysis computes only over already-unblinded reused rows.
new_loopholes.exploit_path: Declare one fresh arm level and log one eligible fresh row; declare comparator reuse for known rows; write the pinned analysis to ignore the fresh row and compute the primary from reused rows. `check_record_reuse` only checks that some declared arm is not covered by reuse blocks, and `verdict-gen` only requires fresh eligibility before passing fresh+reused stdin to the analysis.

new_loopholes.description: Case-A prospective reuse ignores non-final producer data, so exploratory/mock/smoke producer outcomes can be seen before the consumer freeze while the block still passes as prospective.
new_loopholes.exploit_path: Run producer exploratory/non-final rows, inspect outcomes, freeze a consumer `role:prospective` block before final rows/verdict exist, then append matching final producer rows. Freeze checks only final rows and verdict existence.

new_loopholes.description: RC-7 data-blindness is not actually established by code.
new_loopholes.exploit_path: After unblinding, create or choose a file describing the desired comparator rule, set `basis` to one of the allowed enum values, pin its sha, and provide any non-empty `rule_verbatim`. The verifier byte-checks the file but does not prove priority, frozen status, mandatory-law membership, or exhaustiveness.

new_loopholes.description: Verdict-time RC-2 does not require model identity proof, despite the prose and matcher saying corpus plus model identity are required.
new_loopholes.exploit_path: Reuse rows with matching corpus pins but omitted/disjoint/indeterminate model revision pins. `_rc2_pin_identity` only requires at least one shared corpus proof at consumption; model proof is checked only for shared names and can be absent.

new_loopholes.description: Ratification sha binding can be pointed away from the ruling document.
new_loopholes.exploit_path: Commit `registry/reuse-ratification.json` with `plan_path` set to another stable file and matching `plan_sha256`; tools will treat reuse as ratified while later edits to `docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md` do not void it.

new_loopholes.description: Audit/recheck can say a reuse chain verifies when verdict-time RC-2 identity would fail.
new_loopholes.exploit_path: Run `registry-check --reuse` or `reuse-check audit` on a block with placeholder/indeterminate pins. They call `mode="recheck"`, and `_rc2_pin_identity` is invoked with `at_freeze=True`, so consumption-only identity refusals are skipped.

judgment_calls.note: Zero final rows plus no verdict is insufficient. Non-final producer rows can leak outcomes before consumer freeze; later final rows then flow as Case A.
For actual verdict consumption, placeholders fail closed because `mode="verdict"` requires real-vs-real corpus identity. Caveat: append/recheck/audit do not enforce the same consumption semantics.
A single fresh eligible row/arm is not enough unless the primary endpoint is proven to depend on it. Current code does not check analysis dependence on fresh rows.

The pre-spend matcher requires corpus and model for `overall=identical`, and shared encoder alone is correctly insufficient. Verdict-time RC-2 is weaker because absent/disjoint model pins can still pass.
The limited kot-reg/2 scope is acceptable for reuse-specific wiring; the remaining gaps are enforcement logic and ratification binding, not missing D6 fields.

code_vs_policy.note: The tooling enforces many field/row/hash gates, but it is weaker than the prose on RC-7 data-blind priority/substance, RC-4 primary fresh contrast, RC-2 model identity, registry-check collision refusal, and audit/recheck identity semantics.

interlock_fail_closed.note: Today `registry/reuse-ratification.json` is absent; `registry-check --reuse` reports no ratification and `check_record_reuse`/`log-append`/`verdict-gen` require ratification before reuse consumption or witness append. But `reuse_ratified` accepts a ratification `plan_path` other than `docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md`, so editing the ruling doc is not guaranteed to void a ratification.

remaining_amendments: Bind ratification to exactly `docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md` and reject any alternate or absolute `plan_path`; add a schema/test for this.

Strengthen RC-7 to prove priority/frozen status and lawful substance: require a frozen-index/prior log reference or approved immutable law id, verify `rule_verbatim` against the pinned source, and machine-check exhaustiveness where claimed.

For Case A, refuse prospective reuse if the producer has any prior run/output rows for matching cells, not only final rows, or require a frozen no-prior-outcome attestation plus audit-visible quarantine of exploratory data.
Strengthen RC-4 so the frozen primary endpoint/analysis must declare and verify dependence on at least one fresh arm in the primary contrast; dummy fresh rows must not satisfy the gate.

Strengthen verdict-time RC-2 to require all identity-bearing axes applicable to the record type, including model/config/impl/decode/harness pins, not only a shared corpus proof; make recheck/audit use verdict-strength identity semantics once rows exist.

Implement `registry-check --reuse` collision checking or remove the claim; add tests for RC-2 placeholder verdict refusal, missing model pin refusal, Case-A non-final leakage, RC-7 post-unblind ref laundering, CPU-waiver recompute mismatch, ratification wrong-plan-path, and dummy-fresh RC-4 bypass.
reasoning: Read `docs/next/resource-optimization-plan.md`, `docs/next/research-engine.md`, `registry/schema/kot-reg-2.json`, `registry/schema/kot-log-1.json`, `registry/assumptions.jsonl`, `tools/registry/kot_common.py`, `prereg-freeze.py`, `log-append.py`, `verdict-gen.py`, `registry-check.py`, `reuse-check.py`, and `test_fixtures.py`. Refusal paths traced: RC-1 selection rule/complete rows at `kot_common.py:1024-1093`; RC-2 at `868-916` and verdict invocation at `1135-1143`; RC-3 per-item fields at `1031-1034,1144-1149`; RC-4 disclosure/fresh arm at `1050-1082,1178-1194`; RC-5 at `922-1008`; RC-6 witness at `log-append.py:114-147` and `verdict-gen.py:388-426`; RC-7 at `1094-1116`; RC-8 at `1035-1037,1118-1133`. Read-only checks run: `test -f registry/reuse-ratification.json` returned absent; `python3 tools/registry/registry-check.py --reuse` passed with 'no ratification file'; `python3 tools/registry/reuse-check.py lscore` reported 17 free-text/structured mismatches. The 10 TestReuse cases cover several headline refusals, but do not exercise RC-2 consumption placeholders/model absence, Case-A non-final leakage, RC-7 semantic/priority laundering, CPU waiver hash mismatch, wrong ratification plan_path, or dummy-fresh primary bypass.
